Tony goes on to explain to David what Big Daddy's attitude is towards making a mistake. This will give David the directive unconsciously to deal with future mistakes as Big Daddy, not as an injured boy who is wimpy and scared. David is directed to see Big Daddy as being the part of himself for this stage of his life and the part of him that can have fun and get things done.
